reasoning_model_1: The model completely failed the content accuracy criterion. Instead of synthesizing the provided English text ('This precise instrument calibration...'), the model translated both the instruction and the target text into Mandarin Chinese ('开始时语速非常缓慢...'). It read the instructions aloud as part of the speech rather than following them. Due to this fundamental error in language and content generation, the audio receives a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: The model achieved perfect content accuracy, reciting the correct English text. Regarding the paralinguistic requirement to start 'very slow' and 'gradually increase to a medium pace', the model was only partially successful. The first sentence (0:00-0:08) was indeed spoken at a very slow, deliberate pace as requested. However, the model failed to execute the dynamic adjustment for the second sentence (0:08-0:15). The pace remained consistently very slow throughout the entire audio, with distinct pauses between words even in the second half. There was no noticeable acceleration to a 'medium' pace; the pacing feature remained constant. Therefore, while the starting state was correct, the required change was ineffective.
model_comparison: Model 1 (T1) failed completely by generating speech in the wrong language (Chinese translation of the prompt) and reading the instructions aloud. Model 2 (T2) generated the correct English text and successfully established the initial 'very slow' pace. Although T2 failed to dynamically increase the speed to a medium pace as instructed (remaining slow throughout), it is significantly better than T1 because it adhered to the core content script.
